content.claim: In Hungary and Central Europe, Roma children are disproportionately placed in special schools and diagnosed with SEN through documented practices including ethnic-based diagnostic discrimination, with estimates suggesting one in ten Roma children still placed in special or segregated SEN provision, reflecting institutional racism compounding educational segregation
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Historical data (1980s reference) shows past magnitude but current granular data on ethnic composition of SEN rolls not located in source
Documented discrimination reflects systemic patterns but causality between racism and diagnosis is established qualitatively, not quantitatively isolated
European Commission infringement proceedings noted but compliance data on practice change outcomes limited
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
